title: FERG vs. ITW: Which Stock Is the Better Value Option?
text: Investors with an interest in Manufacturing - General Industrial stocks have likely encountered both Ferguson plc (FERG) and Illinois Tool Works (ITW). But which of these two stocks offers value investors a better bang for their buck right now?

title: Here's Why You Should Hold on to Illinois Tool (ITW) Stock
text: Strength in the institutional end markets and capital equipment business augurs well for Illinois Tool (ITW). The company's measures to reward its shareholders are promising.

title: Illinois Tool Works: Great Company, But Not A Value Investment At Current Prices
text: Illinois Tool Works is a company that has consistently increased its dividend payout for over 25 years. The company has shown slow but steady revenue and income growth over the past decade. Share buybacks have helped increase earnings per share and reduce the dividend expense for shareholders.

title: Illinois Tool Works: Good Growth Prospects And A Reasonable Valuation
text: Illinois Tool Works is well positioned for revenue growth and margin expansion, with strong demand for 75% of its business portfolio. The company should be able to post above-market growth thanks to its "customer-back" innovations and increasing market penetration. ITW's margin is expected to benefit from volume leverage, improving price/cost, and operational efficiency efforts, with a long-term target of 30% operating margin by 2030.

title: The 3 Most Undervalued Manufacturing Stocks to Buy in September 2023
text: After years of factory closures and offshoring, manufacturing is returning to America. A 2022 report by the consulting firm McKinsey & Co. said that manufacturing in the U.S. is experiencing a “renaissance”.

title: Dividend Income Summary: Lanny's August 2023 Summary
